summary: Stellar China>Rider, Eyes of the World and jam‑heavy set
blurb: The Greek Theatre show delivers a ripping China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue and an inspired Eyes Of The World that launches into a powerful drum/space segment. Highlights include Hell In A Bucket’s debut, soaring Bird Song, Let It Grow and a monster Throwing Stones. Phil Lesh's bass is massive throughout, making this a must‑listen for any 1983 era fan.
review: May 13, 1983 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre captured the Dead in top form – a night that still feels fresh after all these years. The first set kicked off with a tight run of classics, but it was the inclusion of both Bird Song and Let It Grow that gave the opener an extra lift, showing the band wasn’t holding back any jam‑heavy material. The second set ignited with a glorious China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider combo – reviewers rave about Jerry’s quick guitar fix on the intro and Phil’s “massive bombs” that shake the house. From there the band rode an unstoppable wave through Estimated Prophet into one of the most memorable Eyes Of The World performances of the era, feeding straight into a funny yet ferocious drum/space adventure. Hell In A Bucket made its live debut with blazing energy, while Throwing Stones stretched out into an epic, three‑round solo marathon that left the crowd howling. Across all recordings the sound is crystal clear – whether you prefer the legendary audience tape praised for its “full‑spectrum capture” or the pristine SBD mix – making this show one of the best‑documented Greek Theatre nights.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight recordings are overwhelmingly positive, praising the same standout songs (China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Eyes Of The World, Hell In A Bucket, Throwing Stones) and noting tight playing, massive bass bombs and vibrant drum work; only one mixed review mentions a low guitar mix but does not detract from overall enthusiasm. This consistent acclaim warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: An excellent audience tape hailed as one of the best ever – crystal‑clear detail, no flaws, and captures the full energy of the show.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue with tight playing and massive Phil Lesh bass bombs
Eyes Of The World – blazing solo work and a launch into an unforgettable drum/space segment
Hell In A Bucket debut – high‑energy opening that sets the tone for the night
Throwing Stones – extended three‑round solos creating a monster jam

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers solid, ripping leads – from a quick guitar fix on China Cat Sunflower to soaring solos on Eyes Of The World and Throwing Stones; one SBD mix notes his guitar a bit low but overall his tone blends beautifully with the band.
band_performance.Phil: Phil Lesh is in top form – described as ‘formidable’, ‘invincible’, with massive bass bombs that drive the China Cat segment and a fat, prominent presence throughout the show.
band_performance.Bob: Bob Weir stays engaged, fixing his guitar during the China Cat intro and contributing solid rhythm work; reviewers note his song selection adds to the set’s flow.
band_performance.Brent: Brent Mydland shines on keyboards and vocals, especially on Eyes Of The World where his glowing solos and crisp delivery are repeatedly praised.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey Hart’s contributions to the drum/space section are called funny, powerful and a highlight of the night.
